description_long: This house features a simple, rectangular structure with white concrete block walls and a green metal gable roof. The front section includes a covered porch area supported by white posts, with a brick chimney and a small wood storage nook visible. The main building has a single, small window on the front wall, and the rear section is constructed from the same white blocks. The roof is divided into two sections, with the front portion extending over the porch and the rear section slightly elevated. The house has a utilitarian, unfinished appearance, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on basic shelter. The overall design suggests a modest, functional residence suitable for suburban or rural settings.
description: A modest white concrete block house with a green gable roof, covered porch, and brick chimney. The design is simple and functional, with minimal windows and a utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, small window, covered porch, and green gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended porch roof, brick chimney, and open porch area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain white block wall and the back section of the green roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid white block wall and the full length of the green roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple entry with a covered porch, supported by posts, and no visible decorative elements.
